name: GRANULAR CELL TUMOR
definition: GRANULAR CELL MYOBLASTOMA (misnomer coined in 1926, thought to derive from muscle rare mostly benign neoplasm, occasionally locally invasive + metastasizing

demographics.Histo: nests of ovoid / polygonal cells with small dark regular nuclei and abundant cytoplasm
demographics.Location: tongue (most common), subcutaneous tissue (6-8%), oropharynx, GI tract (5-8%),
demographics.Size: mostly small tumors < 2 cm incidentally discovered at autopsy / endoscopy
demographics.Prevalence: 1÷1,000 of primary breast tumors; 5-8% of all granular cell tumors; 78% in

clinical.Rx: endoscopic / surgical removal if > 1 cm in size
